questions.stem: Under ASC 321, how should an equity investment without a readily determinable fair value be measured if the entity does not elect the fair value option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At cost less impairment, plus or minus observable price changes (measurement alternative)

choices.label: B
choices.text: At lower of cost or market

choices.label: C
choices.text: Using the equity method

choices.label: D
choices.text: At fair value through OCI

questions.explanation: ASC 321-10-35-2 provides a measurement alternative for equity securities without readily determinable fair values. Under this alternative, the investment is measured at cost, minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer.
questions.explanationJa: 容易に決定可能な公正価値のない持分証券には測定の代替(measurement alternative)がある。取得原価 - 減損 +/- 観察可能な価格変動（同一発行者の同一・類似投資の秩序ある取引に基づく）で測定する。

questions.stem: Investor Corp owns 30% of Investee Corp and uses the equity method. Investee reported net income of $200,000 and paid dividends of $50,000. What is the effect on Investor's income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investment income of $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investment income of $45,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dividend income of $15,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Investment income of $60,000 and dividend income of $15,000

questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323-10-35-4), the investor recognizes its share of the investee's net income: 30% × $200,000 = $60,000 as investment income. Dividends received ($50,000 × 30% = $15,000) reduce the investment account but are not recognized as income.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法では被投資会社の純利益に対する持分を認識する。投資収益 = 30% x $200,000 = $60,000。受取配当金 = $50,000 x 30% = $15,000は投資勘定の減額であり、収益ではない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 320, unrealized gains and losses on available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities are reported in:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized until realized

questions.explanation: ASC 320-10-35-1 requires AFS debt securities to be measured at fair value, with unrealized gains and losses excluded from earnings and reported in other comprehensive income (accumulated OCI on the balance sheet). They are reclassified to earnings when sold or impaired.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券は公正価値で測定し、未実現損益は当期利益でなくOCI（その他の包括利益）に計上する。売却時または減損認識時にOCIから当期利益へ振り替える（リサイクリング）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 321, unrealized gains and losses on equity securities with readily determinable fair values are reported in:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: A separate component of equity

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-01 (codified in ASC 321-10-35-1) requires equity securities with readily determinable fair values to be measured at fair value with changes recognized in net income. This eliminated the previous available-for-sale category for equity securities.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-01により、公正価値が容易に決定可能な持分証券は公正価値で測定し、変動を当期純利益に認識する。従来の持分証券のAFS区分は廃止された。負債証券と異なりOCIへの計上は認められない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-30, how is impairment of an available-for-sale debt security recognized when the entity intends to sell the security?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write down to fair value through OCI only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Write down the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value through earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize only the credit loss portion in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: No impairment recognition is required until actual sale

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-4, if the entity intends to sell (or more likely than not will be required to sell) the AFS debt security before recovery, the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value is recognized in earnings. If the entity does not intend to sell, only the credit loss portion is recognized in earnings through an allowance.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券の減損は売却意図の有無で処理が異なる。売却意図あり（または売却を余儀なくされる可能性が高い）: 償却原価とFVの差額全額を当期利益に認識。売却意図なし: 信用損失部分のみ引当金を通じて当期利益に認識し、残りはOCI。

questions.stem: Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported on the balance sheet at:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Amortized cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lower of cost or market

choices.label: D
choices.text: Face value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-25-1, held-to-maturity (HTM) securities are reported at amortized cost. The entity must have the positive intent and ability to hold the security to maturity. Any premium or discount is amortized using the effective interest method.
questions.explanationJa: HTM証券はB/S上で償却原価により報告する。分類要件として、満期まで保有する積極的な意図と能力（positive intent and ability）が必要。プレミアム・ディスカウントは実効金利法で償却する。

questions.stem: Investor A acquired 25% of Company B for $500,000. The book value of Company B's net assets is $1,600,000. The excess of cost over underlying book value is attributed to equipment (fair value exceeds book value by $200,000, 10-year remaining life). What is Investor A's equity method income if Company B reports net income of $300,000?
questions.explanation: Share of net income: 25% × $300,000 = $75,000. Excess cost allocated to equipment: 25% × $200,000 = $50,000, amortized over 10 years = $5,000/year. Equity method income: $75,000 - $5,000 = $70,000.

questions.stem: When a debt security is transferred from held-to-maturity to available-for-sale, the security is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recorded at amortized cost with no adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recorded at the lower of amortized cost or fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10, when a HTM security is transferred to AFS, the security is reclassified at fair value. The unrealized holding gain or loss at the date of transfer is reported in OCI.
questions.explanationJa: HTMからAFSへの振替では、証券を公正価値で再分類する。振替日時点の未実現保有損益はOCIに計上する（当期利益ではない）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 825 (Fair Value Option), an entity elects to measure a financial liability at fair value. Where are subsequent changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-45-5, for financial liabilities measured under the fair value option, changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk are presented separately in OCI. All other changes in fair value are recognized in net income.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値オプション(FVO)で測定する金融負債では、自社の信用リスク(instrument-specific credit risk)に起因するFV変動をOCIに計上する。その他のFV変動は当期純利益に認識する。これにより自社の信用悪化で利益が出る逆説的結果を回避する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 810, which of the following generally requires consolidation of an investee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ownership of 20% or more of voting interests

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ownership of more than 50% of voting interests (controlling financial interest)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Significant influence over the investee

choices.label: D
choices.text: Any equity investment in the investee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, a parent with a controlling financial interest (usually more than 50% of voting interests) must consolidate. 20-50% ownership typically uses the equity method; less than 20% uses fair value under ASC 321.
questions.explanationJa: 支配的持分（通常は議決権50%超）を有する親会社は連結が必要。20-50%の重要な影響力がある場合は持分法。20%未満はASC 321に基づき公正価値（またはmeasurement alternative）で測定。

questions.stem: Investor owns 30% of Investee. Investee reports net income of $600,000. During the year, Investee sold inventory to Investor for $200,000 (cost $140,000). At year-end, 50% of this inventory remains unsold by Investor. What is Investor's equity method income?
questions.explanation: Share of income: 30% × $600,000 = $180,000. Unrealized profit in ending inventory = ($200,000 - $140,000) × 50% = $30,000. Investor's share of unrealized profit: 30% × $30,000 = $9,000. Equity income = $180,000 - $9,000 = $171,000.

questions.stem: Entity R purchases a $100,000 face value bond at $95,000. The bond pays 5% annually and has 5 years to maturity. The effective interest rate is 6.19%. What is interest revenue in Year 1 using the effective interest method?
questions.explanation: Interest revenue = carrying amount × effective rate = $95,000 × 6.19% = $5,881 (rounded). Cash received = $100,000 × 5% = $5,000. Discount amortization = $5,881 - $5,000 = $881. New carrying value = $95,881.

questions.stem: Entity S purchased an AFS debt security for $50,000 (amortized cost). At December 31 Year 1, fair value is $53,000. In Year 2, the security is sold for $54,000. Ignoring amortization, what gain is recognized in Year 2 earnings?
questions.explanation: On sale, the realized gain is measured against amortized cost: $54,000 - $50,000 = $4,000 recognized in earnings. The $3,000 previously in OCI is reclassified to earnings as part of this $4,000 total gain. The additional $1,000 ($54,000 - $53,000) is the Year 2 increment.
questions.explanationJa: 売却時の実現利益は償却原価に対して測定：$54,000 - $50,000 = $4,000が当期利益に認識。OCIに計上されていた$3,000は当期利益に振り替えられる。$1,000（$54,000 - $53,000）はY2の追加増分。

questions.stem: Investor owns 300,000 shares (30%) of Investee (total 1,000,000 shares). Book value of Investee is $3,000,000. Investment carrying value is $900,000. Investee issues 250,000 new shares at $4/share to third parties (book value was $3/share). What gain or loss does Investor recognize on dilution?
questions.explanation: After issuance: Investee BV = $3,000,000 + (250,000 × $4) = $4,000,000. Investor now owns 300,000/1,250,000 = 24%. Investor's share of BV = 24% × $4,000,000 = $960,000. Carrying value was $900,000. Gain on dilution = $960,000 - $900,000 = $60,000.

questions.stem: Entity T purchases a $200,000 face HTM bond at $210,000. Stated rate is 8%, effective rate is 7%. What is interest revenue in Year 2?

Investor carries an equity method investment at $480,000. Due to sustained losses and adverse industry conditions, fair value declines to $420,000 and the decline is deemed other-than-temporary. What is the impairment loss?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-32, if a decline in value of an equity method investment is other-than-temporary, the investment is written down to fair value. Impairment loss = $480,000 - $420,000 = $60,000, recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法投資の価値下落がother-than-temporary（一時的でない）場合、公正価値まで評価減する。減損損失 = $480,000 - $420,000 = $60,000。当期利益に認識し、新たな原価基礎となる（回復しても戻入不可）。

questions.stem: Entity U holds a trading securities portfolio. Cost: $300,000. Fair value at Dec 31 Year 1: $285,000. Fair value at Dec 31 Year 2: $310,000. What unrealized gain or loss is recognized in Year 2 net income?
questions.explanation: Trading securities are marked to fair value each period with changes in net income. Year 2 change = $310,000 - $285,000 = $25,000 unrealized gain. The Year 1 loss of $15,000 was already recognized in Year 1. Each year's income reflects only that year's FV change.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券は毎期公正価値で評価し変動を純利益に認識。Y2の変動 = $310,000 - $285,000 = $25,000の未実現利益。Y1の$15,000損失はY1に既に認識済み。各年度はその年の公正価値変動のみを反映。

questions.stem: Entity V owned 10% of Investee at a cost of $80,000 (FV method). The 10% stake's fair value is now $120,000. Entity V acquires an additional 20% for $240,000, giving it 30% and significant influence. What gain is recognized on the previously held interest at the transition to equity method?
questions.explanation: When transitioning to equity method, the previously held interest is remeasured to fair value. Gain = FV $120,000 - carrying value $80,000 = $40,000. The equity method investment begins at $120,000 + $240,000 = $360,000.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法への移行時、既保有持分を公正価値で再測定する。利益 = FV $120,000 - 帳簿価額$80,000 = $40,000。持分法投資の開始簿価 = 既保有分FV $120,000 + 追加取得$240,000 = $360,000。

questions.stem: Investor holds 40% of Investee using the equity method. Investee reports net income of $300,000 and OCI of $50,000 (foreign currency translation). No dividends were paid. What is the total increase in Investor's investment account?
questions.explanation: Investment account increases by investor's share of both net income and OCI: Income portion: 40% × $300,000 = $120,000 (recognized in investor's NI). OCI portion: 40% × $50,000 = $20,000 (recognized in investor's OCI). Total increase = $120,000 + $20,000 = $140,000.
questions.explanationJa: 投資勘定は純利益とOCI両方の持分で増加：利益部分：40% × $300,000 = $120,000（投資者のNIに認識）。OCI部分：40% × $50,000 = $20,000（投資者のOCIに認識）。合計増加 = $120,000 + $20,000 = $140,000。

questions.stem: Entity W holds a HTM bond at amortized cost of $100,000. Fair value is $92,000. Management estimates expected credit losses of $5,000. What is the net carrying value of the bond after recording the credit loss allowance?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20, HTM securities are measured at amortized cost less an allowance for expected credit losses. Allowance = $5,000. Net carrying value = $100,000 - $5,000 = $95,000. The remaining decline ($8,000 - $5,000 = $3,000) is attributable to market factors, not credit, and is not recognized for HTM.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20に基づき、HTM証券は償却原価から信用損失引当金を控除して測定する。引当金 = $5,000。正味帳簿価額 = $100,000 - $5,000 = $95,000。残りの下落$3,000は信用以外の市場要因によるもので、HTMでは認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity A invests $2,000,000 in a private equity fund that reports net asset value (NAV) per share. The fund has no unfunded commitments and a redemption period of 90 days. Under ASC 820, can Entity A use NAV as a practical expedient for fair value measurement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, if the investment meets the criteria for the NAV practical expedient

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, all investments must use Level 1, 2, or 3 inputs

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only if the fund is publicly traded

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because private equity funds are always Level 3

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-59, entities may use NAV per share as a practical expedient to estimate the fair value of certain investments in funds (including private equity, hedge funds, and real estate funds) that calculate NAV per share consistent with ASC 946. Investments measured using the NAV practical expedient are not categorized within the fair value hierarchy levels.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-59により、ASC 946に準拠してNAVを算出するファンド（プライベートエクイティ、ヘッジファンド、不動産ファンド等）への投資の公正価値を見積もるため、1株当たりNAVを実務上の便法として使用できる。NAV便法で測定された投資は公正価値ヒエラルキーのレベルに分類されない。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities Without Readily Determinable FV
questions.stem: Entity B acquires equity securities of a private company for $500,000. The securities do not have a readily determinable fair value. Entity B does not elect the fair value option. Under ASC 321, how should Entity B measure these securities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At cost, with no subsequent adjustments

choices.label: B
choices.text: At cost minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments

choices.label: C
choices.text: At fair value through net income using Level 3 inputs

choices.label: D
choices.text: At lower of cost or market

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-2, equity securities without readily determinable fair values may use the measurement alternative: cost minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer. This is an election made on an instrument-by-instrument basis.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-2により、容易に決定可能な公正価値のない株式証券は測定代替法を使用できる：取得原価 − 減損 ± 同一発行者の同一または類似投資に関する秩序ある取引における観察可能な価格変動。この選択は金融商品ごとに行う。

questions.stem: Entity C purchases available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities for $200,000. At year-end, the fair value is $215,000. There is no credit loss. What journal entry should Entity C record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Unrealized Gain - Net Income $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Unrealized Gain - OCI $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No entry; AFS securities are held at cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Revenue $15,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-1, AFS debt securities are reported at fair value with unrealized gains and losses (excluding credit losses) reported in other comprehensive income (OCI). The $15,000 unrealized gain ($215,000 − $200,000) is recorded as a credit to OCI, not net income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-1により、AFS負債証券は公正価値で報告し、未実現損益（信用損失を除く）はその他の包括利益（OCI）で報告する。未実現利益$15,000（$215,000 − $200,000）はOCIの貸方に計上し、純利益ではない。

questions.subtopic: Trading Securities - Unrealized vs Realized Gain
questions.stem: Entity D purchases trading securities for $80,000 on March 1. At December 31, the fair value is $95,000. On February 15 of the next year, Entity D sells the securities for $100,000. What gain does Entity D recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Trading securities are marked to fair value through net income. In Year 1, Entity D recognized $15,000 unrealized gain ($95,000 − $80,000) in net income. The carrying value at January 1, Year 2 is $95,000. Upon sale in Year 2, gain = $100,000 − $95,000 = $5,000 recognized in net income.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券は公正価値で測定し変動を純利益に認識する。Year 1に未実現利益$15,000（$95,000 − $80,000）を純利益で認識。Year 2期首の帳簿価額は$95,000。Year 2の売却時の利益 = $100,000 − $95,000 = $5,000を純利益で認識。

questions.stem: Investor E owns 40% of Investee F. Investee F sells inventory to Investor E for $500,000 (cost to F: $300,000). At year-end, 30% of the inventory remains unsold by Investor E. What is the intercompany profit that Investor E must eliminate from its equity method income?
questions.explanation: Intercompany profit = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000. Unsold portion = 30% × $200,000 = $60,000 unrealized profit remaining in inventory. Investor E's share = 40% × $60,000 = $24,000. This $24,000 is eliminated from the investor's share of investee income under the equity method.
questions.explanationJa: グループ間利益 = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000。未販売部分 = 30% × $200,000 = $60,000の未実現利益が棚卸資産に残存。Investor Eの持分 = 40% × $60,000 = $24,000。この$24,000は持分法による投資先利益から消去する。

questions.stem: Entity G purchases a 5-year, $1,000,000 face value bond classified as HTM at $950,000 (discount of $50,000). The stated rate is 6% and the effective rate is 7.1%. What is the interest income in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method (ASC 835-30-35-2), interest income = Carrying amount × Effective rate. Year 1 interest income = $950,000 × 7.1% = $67,450. Cash received = $1,000,000 × 6% = $60,000. Discount amortization = $67,450 − $60,000 = $7,450. New carrying amount = $950,000 + $7,450 = $957,450.

questions.subtopic: Debt Security Reclassification - AFS to HTM
questions.stem: Entity H reclassifies a debt security from AFS to HTM. At the reclassification date, the amortized cost is $100,000, the fair value is $105,000, and there is $5,000 of unrealized gain in AOCI. How is the unrealized gain treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remains in AOCI and amortized over the remaining life as a yield adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminated against retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transferred to a valuation allowance

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10(c), when AFS securities are reclassified to HTM, the security is transferred at fair value ($105,000), which becomes the new amortized cost basis. The unrealized gain ($5,000) in AOCI is not immediately recognized in income but is amortized over the remaining life of the security as a yield adjustment, similar to a premium.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10(c)により、AFS証券をHTMに再分類する場合、公正価値($105,000)で移管し、それが新たな償却原価基準となる。AOCIの未実現利益($5,000)は即時に純利益で認識せず、証券の残存期間にわたりプレミアムと同様に利回り調整として償却する。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Investee Loss Exceeding Investment
questions.stem: Investor J owns 30% of Investee K. Investor J's equity method investment balance is $100,000. Investee K reports a loss of $500,000 for the year. Investor J has no other investments in or commitments to Investee K. What should Investor J recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $150,000 (full 30% share)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $100,000 (reduce investment to zero)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No loss; the investment is already at zero

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-19, if the investor's share of losses ($500,000 × 30% = $150,000) exceeds the carrying amount of the investment ($100,000), the investor recognizes losses only up to the carrying amount, reducing the investment to zero. The investor discontinues applying the equity method. The remaining $50,000 is not recognized unless the investor has guaranteed obligations or is otherwise committed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-19により、投資先損失の持分（$500,000 × 30% = $150,000）が投資の帳簿価額($100,000)を超える場合、投資を零にするまでの損失のみ認識する。残りの$50,000は投資者が債務保証等のコミットメントがない限り認識しない。持分法の適用を中止する。

questions.stem: Entity L holds a corporate bond that is not actively traded. The fair value is estimated using observable market data for similar bonds, adjusted for differences in credit quality and maturity. Under ASC 820, what level in the fair value hierarchy would this bond be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Level 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: Level 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: Level 3

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not categorized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-47, Level 2 inputs include quoted prices for similar assets in active markets or quoted prices for identical assets in markets that are not active. Observable inputs adjusted for differences (like credit quality and maturity) also fall within Level 2. Level 1 requires quoted prices for identical assets in active markets. Level 3 uses unobservable inputs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-47により、レベル2のインプットには活発な市場での類似資産の相場価格や活発でない市場での同一資産の相場価格が含まれる。差異（信用度、満期等）について調整された観察可能なインプットもレベル2に該当する。レベル1は活発な市場での同一資産の相場価格、レベル3は観察不能なインプットを使用する。

questions.stem: Entity M holds equity securities of a private company measured using the measurement alternative (cost minus impairment ± observable price changes). In Year 2, Entity M observes an orderly transaction for similar securities of the same issuer at $30 per share. Entity M's cost was $25 per share for 10,000 shares. What adjustment should Entity M record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a gain in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a credit to OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment; the measurement alternative does not allow upward adjustments

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a credit to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-2, the measurement alternative allows both upward and downward adjustments based on observable price changes. Entity M increases the investment by ($30 − $25) × 10,000 = $50,000 and recognizes a gain of $50,000 in net income. Observable price changes are recognized through earnings, not OCI.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-2により、測定代替法は観察可能な価格変動に基づく上方・下方の両方の調整を認める。Entity Mは投資を($30 − $25) × 10,000 = $50,000増額し、$50,000の利得を純利益で認識する。観察可能な価格変動はOCIではなく損益を通じて認識する。

questions.stem: Entity N purchases a $500,000 face value AFS debt security at $470,000 (discount of $30,000). The security has 3 years to maturity and the effective interest rate is 8.5%. After Year 1, what is the amortized cost?
questions.explanation: Interest income = $470,000 × 8.5% = $39,950. Cash received (assuming annual coupon at stated rate that yields $30,000 discount over 3 years) needs to be determined. With effective rate 8.5%: Interest income = $39,950. Stated coupon = approximately $500,000 × 6% = $30,000. Discount amortization = $39,950 − $30,000 = $9,950. Amortized cost = $470,000 + $9,950 = $479,950.

questions.stem: Investor P initially owns 10% of Investee Q (reported at FV through NI). Investor P acquires an additional 20%, bringing ownership to 30%. The equity method now applies. How should the transition be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the equity method retroactively from the original investment date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the equity method prospectively from the date significant influence is obtained; adjust the existing investment to the new basis

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue reporting the entire investment at fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write off the original 10% investment and start fresh

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-33, when an investor increases its ownership to a level requiring the equity method, the equity method is applied prospectively from the date significant influence is obtained. The carrying amount of the previously held investment becomes part of the equity method investment basis. Any difference between the new cost and the underlying equity is treated as basis differences.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-33により、投資者が持分法が必要な水準まで所有率を増加させた場合、重要な影響力を獲得した日から将来に向けて持分法を適用する。従前保有していた投資の帳簿価額が持分法投資の基礎の一部となる。新たな取得原価と基礎的持分の差額は基礎差額として処理する。

questions.stem: Entity R owns 45% of Entity S's voting stock. Entity S has no other significant shareholders, and Entity R does not control Entity S's board. Under ASC 810, should Entity R consolidate Entity S?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because 45% ownership represents a controlling interest

choices.label: B
choices.text: No; Entity R should use the equity method because it has significant influence but not control

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because ownership exceeds 20%

choices.label: D
choices.text: No; Entity R should report the investment at fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, consolidation is required when a parent has a controlling financial interest, generally through ownership of more than 50% of outstanding voting shares (for a voting interest entity). With 45% and no board control, Entity R has significant influence but not control. The equity method (ASC 323) applies for investments of 20-50% with significant influence.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15-8により、支配的財務持分を有する場合（一般に議決権の50%超の所有）に連結が必要。45%で取締役会の支配がない場合、Entity Rは重要な影響力を有するが支配はしていない。20-50%で重要な影響力がある投資には持分法（ASC 323）が適用される。

questions.stem: Entity T holds trading equity securities with a fair value of $300,000 at the beginning of the year. During the year, Entity T receives cash dividends of $12,000. At year-end, the fair value is $320,000. What total amount should Entity T recognize in net income related to these securities?
questions.explanation: For trading securities, both dividend income and unrealized gains/losses are recognized in net income. Dividend income = $12,000. Unrealized gain = $320,000 − $300,000 = $20,000. Total in net income = $12,000 + $20,000 = $32,000. Dividends and fair value changes are presented separately or together depending on the entity's policy.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券では、配当収入と未実現損益の両方が純利益で認識される。配当収入 = $12,000。未実現利益 = $320,000 − $300,000 = $20,000。純利益の合計 = $12,000 + $20,000 = $32,000。配当と公正価値変動は企業の方針により別個にまたは合算して表示する。

questions.subtopic: AFS Credit Loss vs Non-Credit Loss
questions.stem: Entity U holds an AFS debt security with amortized cost of $200,000 and fair value of $170,000 (decline of $30,000). Entity U determines that $10,000 of the decline is due to credit deterioration and $20,000 is due to changes in interest rates. How should Entity U record this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit loss of $10,000 through an allowance (charged to income); non-credit loss of $20,000 in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total impairment of $30,000 charged to income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total decline of $30,000 recorded in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: No recognition until the security is sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-3, for AFS debt securities, credit losses are recognized through an allowance for credit losses (charged to credit loss expense in income), limited to the amount that fair value is below amortized cost. The non-credit portion of the decline ($20,000) is recognized in OCI. Allowance = $10,000; OCI loss = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-3により、AFS負債証券の信用損失は信用損失引当金（損益での信用損失費用）を通じて認識し、公正価値が償却原価を下回る金額を上限とする。下落の非信用部分($20,000)はOCIで認識する。引当金 = $10,000、OCI損失 = $20,000。

questions.stem: Entity V and Entity W each contribute $1,000,000 to form a 50/50 joint venture (JV). The JV is a separate legal entity and neither party has unilateral control. How should Entity V account for its investment in the JV?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate the JV proportionately

choices.label: B
choices.text: Account for the investment using the equity method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the investment at fair value through net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Account for the investment at cost

questions.explanation: Under US GAAP (ASC 323-10-15-4), corporate joint ventures in which the investor has significant influence (typically 20-50% ownership) are accounted for using the equity method. Proportionate consolidation is not permitted under US GAAP (though it is allowed under IFRS in limited circumstances). Neither party controls the JV, so full consolidation is not appropriate.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 323-10-15-4）では、投資者が重要な影響力を有する共同事業体（通常20-50%の所有）は持分法で会計処理する。US GAAPでは比例連結は認められない（IFRSでは限定的に許容）。いずれの当事者もJVを支配していないため、完全連結は適切でない。

questions.stem: Investor X paid $2,000,000 for a 25% interest in Investee Y. Investee Y's identifiable net assets at acquisition had a fair value of $6,000,000. The implied goodwill in the equity method investment is being reviewed. What is the goodwill embedded in Investor X's investment?
questions.explanation: Investor X's share of identifiable net assets = 25% × $6,000,000 = $1,500,000. Cost of investment = $2,000,000. Goodwill embedded in the investment = $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000. Under the equity method, goodwill is not separately tested for impairment; instead, the entire investment is tested under ASC 323-10-35-32.
questions.explanationJa: Investor Xの識別可能純資産の持分 = 25% × $6,000,000 = $1,500,000。投資の取得原価 = $2,000,000。投資に含まれるのれん = $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000。持分法では、のれんは個別に減損テストせず、投資全体をASC 323-10-35-32に基づきテストする。

questions.subtopic: Debt Security Interest Income at Acquisition
questions.stem: Entity Z purchases a bond between interest dates on April 1 for $1,030,000, which includes $30,000 of accrued interest. The face value is $1,000,000. What is the cost basis of the bond?
questions.explanation: When purchasing a bond between interest dates, the buyer pays the seller for accrued interest since the last interest payment date. The accrued interest ($30,000) is recorded as interest receivable, not as part of the bond's cost. The cost basis of the bond = $1,030,000 − $30,000 = $1,000,000 (at par in this case).
questions.explanationJa: 利払日の間に社債を購入する場合、買い手は前回の利払日からの経過利息を売り手に支払う。経過利息($30,000)は未収利息として計上し、社債の取得原価には含めない。社債の取得原価 = $1,030,000 − $30,000 = $1,000,000（この場合は額面価額）。

questions.stem: Entity A reclassifies a debt security from AFS to trading. At the reclassification date, the amortized cost is $90,000 and the fair value is $95,000. There is $5,000 of unrealized gain in AOCI. How is the transfer accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The $5,000 unrealized gain in AOCI is reclassified to net income immediately

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $5,000 remains in AOCI and is amortized

choices.label: C
choices.text: The security is transferred at amortized cost; no gain recognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: The $5,000 is recorded as a direct adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10(b), when AFS securities are transferred to trading, the unrealized gain or loss in AOCI at the date of transfer is immediately reclassified and recognized in net income. The security is carried at fair value ($95,000) in the trading portfolio going forward. The $5,000 AOCI gain is recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10(b)により、AFS証券をトレーディングに移管する場合、移管日時点のAOCIの未実現損益は即時に純利益に再分類して認識する。証券は今後トレーディング・ポートフォリオで公正価値($95,000)で計上する。AOCIの$5,000の利得は損益で認識する。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Through Net Income Election
questions.stem: Entity B has a debt instrument that would normally be classified as AFS. Entity B elects the fair value option under ASC 825 at initial recognition. How are subsequent changes in fair value reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: In net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: In retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: No subsequent measurement is required

questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-45-2, when the fair value option is elected, subsequent changes in fair value are recognized in net income. This is the case regardless of the original classification. The election is irrevocable and made on an instrument-by-instrument basis. The entity must disclose the reasons for electing the fair value option.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 825-10-45-2により、公正価値オプションを選択した場合、その後の公正価値変動は純利益で認識する。これは当初の分類にかかわらず適用される。選択は取消不能で金融商品ごとに行う。企業は公正価値オプション選択の理由を開示しなければならない。
